tools.comment: Reads the complete contents of a file as text, with optional head/tail line limiting (deprecated in favor of read_text_file).

tail.description: If provided, returns only the last N lines of the file

head.description: If provided, returns only the first N lines of the file

tools.comment: Reads the complete contents of a text file from the filesystem, with optional head/tail line limiting.

tail.description: If provided, returns only the last N lines of the file

head.description: If provided, returns only the first N lines of the file

tools.comment: Reads an image or audio file and returns its base64-encoded data and MIME type.
Reads the contents of multiple files simultaneously, returning each file's content keyed by its path.

paths.description: Array of file paths to read. Each path must be a string pointing to a valid file within allowed directories.

tools.comment: Creates a new file or completely overwrites an existing file with the specified text content.
Makes line-based edits to a text file by replacing exact line sequences, with optional dry-run preview.

oldText.description: Text to search for - must match exactly

dryRun.description: Preview changes using git-style diff format

tools.comment: Creates a new directory (including nested directories) or silently succeeds if it already exists.
Returns a detailed listing of all files and directories within a specified directory path.
Returns a detailed listing of all files and directories in a path including their sizes, with optional sorting.

sortBy.description: Sort entries by name or size

tools.comment: Returns a recursive JSON tree view of files and directories under a given path, with optional exclusion patterns.
Moves or renames a file or directory from a source path to a destination path in a single operation.
Recursively searches for files and directories matching a glob-style pattern within a given directory.
Retrieves detailed metadata (size, timestamps, permissions, type) about a file or directory without reading its content.
Returns the list of directories that the server is permitted to access.
Stages specified files or all changes in the repository for the next commit.

path.description: Path to the Git repository. Defaults to session working directory set via git_set_working_dir.

files.description: Array of file paths to stage (relative to repository root). Use ["."] to stage all changes.

update.description: Stage only modified and deleted files (skip untracked files).

tools.comment: Shows line-by-line authorship information for a file, indicating who last modified each line and when.

path.description: Path to the Git repository. Defaults to session working directory set via git_set_working_dir.

file.description: Path to the file to blame (relative to repository root).

tools.comment: Manages git branches by listing, creating, deleting, renaming, or showing the current branch.

path.description: Path to the Git repository. Defaults to session working directory set via git_set_working_dir.

newName.description: New branch name for rename operation.

startPoint.description: Starting point (commit/branch) for new branch creation.

force.description: Force the operation, bypassing safety checks.

all.description: For list operation: show both local and remote branches.

remote.description: For list operation: show only remote branches.

merged.description: For list operation: show only branches merged into HEAD (true) or specified commit (string).

anyOf.description: Commit reference: full/short hash, branch name, tag name, or relative ref (HEAD~1).

noMerged.description: For list operation: show only branches not merged into HEAD (true) or specified commit (string).

anyOf.description: Commit reference: full/short hash, branch name, tag name, or relative ref (HEAD~1).

tools.comment: Gathers git history context and structured review instructions to support LLM-driven changelog analysis.

path.description: Path to the Git repository. Defaults to session working directory set via git_set_working_dir.

reviewTypes.description: Types of changelog review to perform. At least one required. Options: security, features, storyline, gaps, breaking_changes, quality.

maxCommits.description: Maximum recent commits to fetch for cross-referencing (1-1000).

sinceTag.description: Only include git history since this tag (e.g., "v1.2.0"). Narrows the analysis window.

branch.description: Branch to analyze (defaults to current branch).

tools.comment: Switches branches, creates new branches, or restores specific working tree files to a given state.

path.description: Path to the Git repository. Defaults to session working directory set via git_set_working_dir.

anyOf.description: Branch name (must follow git naming conventions).
Commit reference: full/short hash, branch name, tag name, or relative ref (HEAD~1).

target.description: Branch name, commit hash, or tag to checkout.

createBranch.description: Create a new branch with the specified name.

force.description: Force the operation, bypassing safety checks.

paths.description: Specific file paths to checkout/restore (relative to repository root).

track.description: Set up tracking relationship with remote branch when creating new branch.

tools.comment: Applies specific commits from other branches onto the current branch without merging entire branches.

path.description: Path to the Git repository. Defaults to session working directory set via git_set_working_dir.

items.description: Commit reference: full/short hash, branch name, tag name, or relative ref (HEAD~1).

mainline.description: For merge commits, specify which parent to follow (1 for first parent, 2 for second, etc.).

strategy.description: Merge strategy to use for cherry-pick.

signoff.description: Add Signed-off-by line to the commit message.

tools.comment: Removes untracked files (and optionally directories) from the working directory, with dry-run preview support.

path.description: Path to the Git repository. Defaults to session working directory set via git_set_working_dir.

force.description: Force the operation, bypassing safety checks.

dryRun.description: Preview the operation without executing it.

directories.description: Remove untracked directories in addition to files.

tools.comment: Clears the session working directory setting, requiring subsequent git operations to supply an explicit path.

confirm.description: Explicit confirmation required to clear working directory. Accepted values: 'Y', 'y', 'Yes', or 'yes'.

tools.comment: Clones a remote git repository to a local path, with support for shallow cloning and bare/mirror options.

url.description: Remote repository URL to clone from.

localPath.description: Local path where the repository should be cloned.

branch.description: Specific branch to clone (defaults to remote HEAD).

depth.description: Create a shallow clone with history truncated to N commits.

bare.description: Create a bare repository (no working directory).

tools.comment: Creates a new commit recording staged changes in the repository with the provided commit message.

path.description: Path to the Git repository. Defaults to session working directory set via git_set_working_dir.

author.description: Override commit author (defaults to git config).

amend.description: Amend the previous commit instead of creating a new one. Use with caution.

allowEmpty.description: Allow creating a commit with no changes.

filesToStage.description: File paths to stage before committing (atomic stage+commit operation).

forceUnsignedOnFailure.description: If GPG/SSH signing fails, retry the commit without signing instead of failing.

tools.comment: Shows differences between commits, branches, the index, or the working tree in unified diff format.

path.description: Path to the Git repository. Defaults to session working directory set via git_set_working_dir.

target.description: Target commit/branch to compare against. If not specified, shows unstaged changes in working tree.

source.description: Source commit/branch to compare from. If target is specified but not source, compares target against working tree.

paths.description: Limit diff to specific file paths (relative to repository root).

staged.description: Show diff of staged changes instead of unstaged.

includeUntracked.description: Include untracked files in the diff. Useful for reviewing all upcoming changes.

nameOnly.description: Show only names of changed files, not the diff content.

stat.description: Show diffstat (summary of changes) instead of full diff content.

contextLines.description: Number of context lines to show around changes.

tools.comment: Downloads objects and refs from a remote repository without merging them into the local branch.

path.description: Path to the Git repository. Defaults to session working directory set via git_set_working_dir.

prune.description: Prune remote-tracking references that no longer exist on remote.

tags.description: Fetch all tags from the remote.

depth.description: Create a shallow clone with history truncated to N commits.

tools.comment: Initializes a new git repository at the specified path, creating the .git directory and initial branch.

path.description: Path to the Git repository. Defaults to session working directory set via git_set_working_dir.

initialBranch.description: Name of the initial branch (default: main).

bare.description: Create a bare repository (no working directory).

tools.comment: Displays commit history with optional filtering by author, date range, file path, or commit message pattern.

path.description: Path to the Git repository. Defaults to session working directory set via git_set_working_dir.

maxCount.description: Maximum number of items to return (1-1000).

skip.description: Number of items to skip for pagination.

since.description: Show commits more recent than a specific date (ISO 8601 format).

until.description: Show commits older than a specific date (ISO 8601 format).

author.description: Filter commits by author name or email pattern.

grep.description: Filter commits by message pattern (regex supported).

branch.description: Show commits from a specific branch or ref (defaults to current branch).

filePath.description: Show commits that affected a specific file path.

oneline.description: Show each commit on a single line (abbreviated output).

stat.description: Include file change statistics for each commit.

patch.description: Include the full diff patch for each commit.

showSignature.description: Show GPG signature verification information for each commit.

tools.comment: Merges another branch into the current branch, with optional merge strategy and commit message.

path.description: Path to the Git repository. Defaults to session working directory set via git_set_working_dir.

branch.description: Branch to merge into current branch.

strategy.description: Merge strategy to use (ort, recursive, octopus, ours, subtree).

squash.description: Squash all commits from the branch into a single commit.

abort.description: Abort an in-progress merge that has conflicts.

tools.comment: Fetches and integrates changes from a remote repository into the current local branch.

path.description: Path to the Git repository. Defaults to session working directory set via git_set_working_dir.

rebase.description: Use rebase instead of merge when integrating changes.

fastForwardOnly.description: Fail if can't fast-forward (no merge commit).

tools.comment: Uploads local commits to a remote repository branch, with support for force push and branch deletion.

path.description: Path to the Git repository. Defaults to session working directory set via git_set_working_dir.

forceWithLease.description: Safer force push - only succeeds if remote branch is at expected state.

setUpstream.description: Set upstream tracking relationship for the branch.

tags.description: Push all tags to the remote.

dryRun.description: Preview the operation without executing it.

remoteBranch.description: Remote branch name to push to (if different from local branch name).

confirmed.description: Explicit confirmation required for force push or branch deletion on protected branches (main, master, production, etc.).

tools.comment: Reapplies commits on top of another base tip to produce a cleaner linear history.

path.description: Path to the Git repository. Defaults to session working directory set via git_set_working_dir.

upstream.description: Upstream branch to rebase onto (required for start mode).

interactive.description: Interactive rebase (not supported in all providers).

onto.description: Rebase onto different commit than upstream.

tools.comment: Displays the reference logs showing when branch tips and other refs were updated, useful for recovering lost commits.

path.description: Path to the Git repository. Defaults to session working directory set via git_set_working_dir.

ref.description: Show reflog for specific reference (default: HEAD).

maxCount.description: Maximum number of items to return (1-1000).

tools.comment: Manages git remote repositories by listing, adding, removing, renaming, or getting/setting remote URLs.

path.description: Path to the Git repository. Defaults to session working directory set via git_set_working_dir.

newName.description: New remote name for rename operation.

push.description: Set push URL separately (for set-url operation).

tools.comment: Resets the current HEAD to a specified state, optionally unstaging files or discarding working tree changes.

path.description: Path to the Git repository. Defaults to session working directory set via git_set_working_dir.

mode.description: Reset mode: soft (keep changes staged), mixed (unstage changes), hard (discard all changes), merge (reset and merge), keep (reset but keep local changes).

target.description: Target commit to reset to (default: HEAD).

paths.description: Specific file paths to reset (leaves HEAD unchanged).

confirmed.description: Explicit confirmation required for hard reset on protected branches (main, master, production, etc.).

tools.comment: Sets the session-level default working directory for all subsequent git operations.

path.description: Absolute path to the git repository to use as the working directory.

validateGitRepo.description: Validate that the path is a Git repository.

initializeIfNotPresent.description: If not a Git repository, initialize it with 'git init'.

includeMetadata.description: Include repository metadata (status, branches, remotes, recent commits) in the response. Set to true for immediate repository context understanding. Defaults to false to minimize response size.

tools.comment: Displays details and diff of a git object such as a commit, tree, blob, or tag.

path.description: Path to the Git repository. Defaults to session working directory set via git_set_working_dir.

object.description: Git object to show (commit hash, branch, tag, tree, or blob).

format.description: Output format for the git object.

stat.description: Show diffstat instead of full diff.

filePath.description: View specific file at a given commit reference. When provided, shows the file content from the specified object.

tools.comment: Manages stashes by listing, saving, restoring, or removing stashed working tree changes.

path.description: Path to the Git repository. Defaults to session working directory set via git_set_working_dir.

stashRef.description: Stash reference like stash@{0} (for pop/apply/drop operations).

includeUntracked.description: Include untracked files in the stash (for push operation).

keepIndex.description: Don't revert staged changes (for push operation).

tools.comment: Shows the current working tree status including staged, unstaged, and untracked files.

path.description: Path to the Git repository. Defaults to session working directory set via git_set_working_dir.

includeUntracked.description: Include untracked files in the output.

tools.comment: Manages git tags by listing all tags, creating a new tag, or deleting an existing tag at a specific commit.

path.description: Path to the Git repository. Defaults to session working directory set via git_set_working_dir.

commit.description: Commit to tag (default: HEAD for create operation).

forceUnsignedOnFailure.description: If GPG/SSH signing fails, retry the tag creation without signing instead of failing.

tools.comment: Manages git worktrees by listing, adding, removing, moving, or pruning working trees associated with a repository.

path.description: Path to the Git repository. Defaults to session working directory set via git_set_working_dir.

worktreePath.description: Path for the new worktree (for add/move operations).

branch.description: Branch to checkout in the new worktree (for add operation).

commitish.description: Commit/branch to base the worktree on (for add operation).

force.description: Force operation (for remove operation with uncommitted changes).

newPath.description: New path for the worktree (for move operation).

detach.description: Create worktree with detached HEAD (for add operation).

verbose.description: Provide detailed output for worktree operations.

dryRun.description: Preview the operation without executing it (for prune operation).

tools.comment: Returns the user's configured Git wrap-up workflow instructions or default best practices for reviewing, documenting, and committing changes.

acknowledgement.description: Acknowledgement to initiate the wrap-up workflow.

updateAgentMetaFiles.description: Include an instruction to update agent-specific meta files.

createTag.description: If true, instructs the agent to create a Git tag after committing all changes. Only set to true if given permission to do so.

tools.comment: Fetches the content of a given URL via HTTP GET and returns the status, headers, and body in a specified format.

max_length.description: Maximum response body length in characters

format.description: Output format: markdown (default), text (stripped tags), or html (raw)

tools.comment: Searches the web using a configured search provider and returns a list of results for the given query.

max_results.description: Maximum number of results to return (1–20)

tools.comment: Adds a review comment to the requester's latest pending pull request review.

body.description: The text of the review comment

line.description: The line of the blob in the pull request diff that the comment applies to. For multi-line comments, the last line of the range

path.description: The relative path to the file that necessitates a comment

side.description: The side of the diff to comment on. LEFT indicates the previous state, RIGHT indicates the new state

startLine.description: For multi-line comments, the first line of the range that the comment applies to

startSide.description: For multi-line comments, the starting side of the diff that the comment applies to. LEFT indicates the previous state, RIGHT indicates the new state

subjectType.description: The level at which the comment is targeted

tools.comment: Adds a comment to a specific issue or pull request in a GitHub repository.
Adds a reply to an existing pull request comment by comment ID.

commentId.description: The ID of the comment to reply to

tools.comment: Assigns GitHub Copilot as an agent to work on a specific issue and potentially create a pull request with fixes.

base_ref.description: Git reference (e.g., branch) that the agent will start its work from. If not specified, defaults to the repository's default branch

custom_instructions.description: Optional custom instructions to guide the agent beyond the issue body. Use this to provide additional context, constraints, or guidance that is not captured in the issue description

tools.comment: Creates a new branch in a GitHub repository, optionally from a specified source branch.
Creates or updates a single file in a GitHub repository on a specified branch.

branch.description: Branch to create/update the file in

path.description: Path where to create/update the file

sha.description: The blob SHA of the file being replaced. Required if the file already exists.

tools.comment: Creates a new pull request in a GitHub repository from a head branch into a base branch.
Creates a new GitHub repository in a personal account or specified organization.

organization.description: Organization to create the repository in (omit to create in your personal account)

tools.comment: Deletes a file from a specified branch in a GitHub repository.

branch.description: Branch to delete the file from

path.description: Path to the file to delete

tools.comment: Forks a GitHub repository to the authenticated user's account or a specified organization.
Retrieves details for a specific commit from a GitHub repository by SHA, branch, or tag.

include_diff.description: Whether to include file diffs and stats in the response. Default is true.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

sha.description: Commit SHA, branch name, or tag name

tools.comment: Retrieves the contents of a file or directory from a GitHub repository.

path.description: Path to file/directory

ref.description: Accepts optional git refs such as `refs/tags/{tag}`, `refs/heads/{branch}` or `refs/pull/{pr_number}/head`

sha.description: Accepts optional commit SHA. If specified, it will be used instead of ref

tools.comment: Retrieves details of a specific label from a GitHub repository.
Retrieves the latest release from a GitHub repository.
Retrieves profile details of the currently authenticated GitHub user.
Retrieves a specific release by its tag name from a GitHub repository.
Retrieves details about a specific git tag in a GitHub repository.
Retrieves the member usernames of a specific team within a GitHub organization.

org.description: Organization login (owner) that contains the team.

tools.comment: Retrieves details of the teams the specified or authenticated user is a member of.

user.description: Username to get teams for. If not provided, uses the authenticated user.

tools.comment: Reads information about a specific issue in a GitHub repository, including details, comments, sub-issues, or labels.

method.description:
The read operation to perform on a single issue.
Options are:
1. get - Get details of a specific issue.
2. get_comments - Get issue comments.
3. get_sub_issues - Get sub-issues of the issue.
4. get_labels - Get labels assigned to the issue.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.comment: Creates a new issue or updates an existing issue in a GitHub repository.

assignees.description: Usernames to assign to this issue

duplicate_of.description: Issue number that this issue is a duplicate of. Only used when state_reason is 'duplicate'.

labels.description: Labels to apply to this issue

method.description:
Write operation to perform on a single issue.
Options are:
- 'create' - creates a new issue.
- 'update' - updates an existing issue.

state_reason.description: Reason for the state change. Ignored unless state is changed.

type.description: Type of this issue. Only use if the repository has issue types configured. Use list_issue_types tool to get valid type values for the organization. If the repository doesn't support issue types, omit this parameter.

tools.comment: Lists branches in a GitHub repository with pagination support.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.comment: Lists commits on a branch or up to a specific SHA in a GitHub repository.

author.description: Author username or email address to filter commits by

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

sha.description: Commit SHA, branch or tag name to list commits of. If not provided, uses the default branch of the repository. If a commit SHA is provided, will list commits up to that SHA.

tools.comment: Lists the supported issue types configured for a GitHub organization.

owner.description: The organization owner of the repository

tools.comment: Lists issues in a GitHub repository with optional filtering by state, labels, and ordering.

after.description: Cursor for pagination. Use the endCursor from the previous page's PageInfo for GraphQL APIs.

direction.description: Order direction. If provided, the 'orderBy' also needs to be provided.

orderBy.description: Order issues by field. If provided, the 'direction' also needs to be provided.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

state.description: Filter by state, by default both open and closed issues are returned when not provided

tools.comment: Lists pull requests in a GitHub repository with optional filters for state, branch, and sorting.

head.description: Filter by head user/org and branch

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.comment: Lists releases published in a GitHub repository.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.comment: Lists git tags in a GitHub repository.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.comment: Merges a pull request in a GitHub repository using the specified merge method.
Retrieves various read-only information about a specific pull request, such as details, diff, status, files, comments, reviews, and check runs.

method.description:
Action to specify what pull request data needs to be retrieved from GitHub. 
Possible options: 
 1. get - Get details of a specific pull request.
 2. get_diff - Get the diff of a pull request.
 3. get_status - Get combined commit status of a head commit in a pull request.
 4. get_files - Get the list of files changed in a pull request. Use with pagination parameters to control the number of results returned.
 5. get_review_comments - Get review threads on a pull request. Each thread contains logically grouped review comments made on the same code location during pull request reviews. Returns threads with metadata (isResolved, isOutdated, isCollapsed) and their associated comments. Use cursor-based pagination (perPage, after) to control results.
 6. get_reviews - Get the reviews on a pull request. When asked for review comments, use get_review_comments method.
 7. get_comments - Get comments on a pull request. Use this if user doesn't specifically want review comments. Use with pagination parameters to control the number of results returned.
 8. get_check_runs - Get check runs for the head commit of a pull request. Check runs are the individual CI/CD jobs and checks that run on the PR.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.comment: Creates, submits, or deletes a review on a pull request in a GitHub repository.

method.description: The write operation to perform on pull request review.

tools.comment: Pushes multiple files to a GitHub repository branch in a single commit.

path.description: path to the file

files.description: Array of file objects to push, each object with path (string) and content (string)

tools.comment: Requests an automated GitHub Copilot code review for a specific pull request.
Searches for code across GitHub repositories using GitHub's native code search syntax.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Search query using GitHub's powerful code search syntax. Examples: 'content:Skill language:Java org:github', 'NOT is:archived language:Python OR language:go', 'repo:github/github-mcp-server'. Supports exact matching, language filters, path filters, and more.

tools.comment: Searches for issues across GitHub repositories using GitHub issues search syntax.

owner.description: Optional repository owner. If provided with repo, only issues for this repository are listed.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Search query using GitHub issues search syntax

repo.description: Optional repository name. If provided with owner, only issues for this repository are listed.

sort.description: Sort field by number of matches of categories, defaults to best match

tools.comment: Searches for pull requests across GitHub repositories using GitHub pull request search syntax.

owner.description: Optional repository owner. If provided with repo, only pull requests for this repository are listed.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Search query using GitHub pull request search syntax

repo.description: Optional repository name. If provided with owner, only pull requests for this repository are listed.

sort.description: Sort field by number of matches of categories, defaults to best match

tools.comment: Searches for GitHub repositories by name, description, topics, or other metadata.

minimal_output.description: Return minimal repository information (default: true). When false, returns full GitHub API repository objects.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Repository search query. Examples: 'machine learning in:name stars:>1000 language:python', 'topic:react', 'user:facebook'. Supports advanced search syntax for precise filtering.

sort.description: Sort repositories by field, defaults to best match

tools.comment: Searches for GitHub users by username, real name, or other profile attributes.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: User search query. Examples: 'john smith', 'location:seattle', 'followers:>100'. Search is automatically scoped to type:user.

sort.description: Sort users by number of followers or repositories, or when the person joined GitHub.

tools.comment: Adds, removes, or reprioritizes a sub-issue relationship under a parent issue in a GitHub repository.

after_id.description: The ID of the sub-issue to be prioritized after (either after_id OR before_id should be specified)

before_id.description: The ID of the sub-issue to be prioritized before (either after_id OR before_id should be specified)

issue_number.description: The number of the parent issue

method.description:
The action to perform on a single sub-issue
Options are:
- 'add' - add a sub-issue to a parent issue in a GitHub repository.
- 'remove' - remove a sub-issue from a parent issue in a GitHub repository.
- 'reprioritize' - change the order of sub-issues within a parent issue in a GitHub repository. Use either 'after_id' or 'before_id' to specify the new position.

replace_parent.description: When true, replaces the sub-issue's current parent issue. Use with 'add' method only.

sub_issue_id.description: The ID of the sub-issue to add. ID is not the same as issue number

tools.comment: Updates metadata and settings of an existing pull request in a GitHub repository.

draft.description: Mark pull request as draft (true) or ready for review (false)

reviewers.description: GitHub usernames to request reviews from

tools.comment: Updates the head branch of a pull request with the latest changes from its base branch.

expectedHeadSha.description: The expected SHA of the pull request's HEAD ref

tools.comment: Searches for files in Google Drive using a query string.

pageToken.description: Token for the next page of results

tools.comment: Reads and returns the contents of a file from Google Drive by file ID.

fileId.description: ID of the file to read

tools.comment: Lists files in Google Drive with optional filtering, pagination, and sorting.

folderId.description: Folder ID to list files from (limits search to specific folder)

pageSize.description: Number of files to return per page (max 1000, default 100)

tools.comment: Retrieves detailed metadata for a specific file in Google Drive.

fileId.description: ID of the file to get metadata for

fields.description: Comma-separated list of fields to include (default: all common fields)

tools.comment: Uploads a new file with content to Google Drive.

content.description: File content (base64 encoded for binary files, plain text for text files)

parents.description: Parent folder ID(s) where the file should be created

tools.comment: Creates an empty file or Google Workspace document in Google Drive without content.

parents.description: Parent folder ID(s) where the file should be created

tools.comment: Moves a file to trash in Google Drive by file ID.

fileId.description: ID of the file to delete

tools.comment: Creates a duplicate of a file in Google Drive with an optional new name and location.

fileId.description: ID of the file to copy

name.description: Name for the copied file (optional, defaults to 'Copy of [original name]')

parents.description: Parent folder ID(s) for the copied file (optional, defaults to same location as original)

tools.comment: Moves a file to a different folder in Google Drive by updating its parent folder(s).

fileId.description: ID of the file to move

newParents.description: New parent folder ID(s) to move the file to

removeParents.description: Parent folder ID(s) to remove the file from (optional, if not specified, removes from all current parents)

tools.comment: Renames a file in Google Drive while keeping all other properties unchanged.

tools.comment: Updates a file's metadata and/or content in Google Drive.

fileId.description: ID of the file to update

content.description: New file content (base64 encoded for binary files, plain text for text files) (optional)

tools.comment: Creates a new folder in Google Drive, optionally inside a specified parent folder.

tools.comment: Lists all files and folders inside a specific Google Drive folder.

folderId.description: ID of the folder to list contents from

pageSize.description: Number of items to return per page (max 1000, default 100)

tools.comment: Moves a file into a specified destination folder in Google Drive.

fileId.description: ID of the file to move

tools.comment: Shares a Google Drive file by adding permissions for a user, group, domain, or anyone with the link.

fileId.description: ID of the file to share

role.description: Permission role: owner (full control), writer (editor), commenter (comment only), reader (viewer)

type.description: Type of recipient: user (email), group (group email), domain (entire domain), anyone (anyone with link)

emailAddress.description: Email address (required for type=user or type=group)

sendNotificationEmail.description: Send notification email to the recipient(s). Default: true

emailMessage.description: Custom message to include in notification email

tools.comment: Lists all permissions for a Google Drive file, showing who has access and at what level.

fileId.description: ID of the file to get permissions for

tools.comment: Updates an existing permission's role on a Google Drive file.

fileId.description: ID of the file whose permission to update

permissionId.description: ID of the permission to update (get from drive_list_permissions)

role.description: New permission role: owner (full control), writer (editor), commenter (comment only), reader (viewer)

tools.comment: Removes a permission from a Google Drive file, revoking the associated user or group's access.

fileId.description: ID of the file to remove permission from

permissionId.description: ID of the permission to remove (get from drive_list_permissions)

tools.comment: Exports a Google Workspace document to a specified format such as PDF, DOCX, or CSV.

fileId.description: ID of the file to export

mimeType.description: Target MIME type for export. Optional - uses default if not specified. Examples: application/pdf, text/csv, text/plain, etc.

format.description: Export format name (shorthand). For Docs: pdf, docx, html, txt, odt, rtf, epub, markdown. For Sheets: csv, xlsx, ods, tsv, html. For Slides: pdf, pptx, odp, txt. For Drawing: png, jpeg, svg, pdf.

tools.comment: Lists all revisions of a file in Google Drive with timestamps and modification details.

fileId.description: ID of the file to list revisions for

pageSize.description: Maximum number of revisions to return per page (default: 10, max: 1000)

pageToken.description: Token for pagination to get the next page of results

tools.comment: Permanently deletes all files in the authenticated user's Google Drive trash.
Updates the value of a specific cell in a Google Spreadsheet.
Reads data from a Google Spreadsheet with optional range and sheet filtering.

spreadsheetId.description: The ID of the spreadsheet to read

ranges.description: Optional array of A1 notation ranges like ['Sheet1!A1:B10']. If not provided, reads entire sheet.

sheetId.description: Optional specific sheet ID to read. If not provided with ranges, reads first sheet.

tools.comment: Lists all sheets (tabs) within a Google Spreadsheet.
Adds a new sheet (tab) to a Google Spreadsheet at an optional position.

title.description: The name of the new sheet

index.description: Optional position index (0-based) where the sheet should be inserted

tools.comment: Deletes a specific sheet (tab) from a Google Spreadsheet by its ID.

sheetId.description: The ID of the sheet to delete

tools.comment: Inserts a specified number of empty rows at a given index in a Google Spreadsheet sheet.

startIndex.description: The starting row index (0-based) where rows will be inserted

count.description: The number of rows to insert

tools.comment: Inserts a specified number of empty columns at a given index in a Google Spreadsheet sheet.

startIndex.description: The starting column index (0-based) where columns will be inserted

count.description: The number of columns to insert

tools.comment: Deletes a specified number of rows starting at a given index from a Google Spreadsheet sheet.

startIndex.description: The starting row index (0-based) to delete

count.description: The number of rows to delete

tools.comment: Deletes a specified number of columns starting at a given index from a Google Spreadsheet sheet.

startIndex.description: The starting column index (0-based) to delete

count.description: The number of columns to delete

tools.comment: Appends rows of data to the end of a specified range in a Google Spreadsheet.

range.description: The A1 notation range to append to (e.g., 'Sheet1!A:B')

valueInputOption.description: How input data should be interpreted (RAW or USER_ENTERED). Default: RAW

tools.comment: Updates multiple cell ranges with new values in a single batch operation on a Google Spreadsheet.

updates.description: Array of range-value pairs to update

valueInputOption.description: How input data should be interpreted (RAW or USER_ENTERED). Default: RAW

tools.comment: Clears all data from one or more specified ranges in a Google Spreadsheet.

ranges.description: Array of A1 notation ranges to clear (e.g., ['Sheet1!A1:B10', 'Sheet2!C1:D5'])

tools.comment: Copies a sheet from one Google Spreadsheet to another (or within the same spreadsheet).

sourceSpreadsheetId.description: The ID of the source spreadsheet

sourceSheetId.description: The ID of the sheet to copy

destinationSpreadsheetId.description: The ID of the destination spreadsheet

tools.comment: Copies data from one range to another range within a Google Spreadsheet using cut/paste.

sourceSheetId.description: The ID of the source sheet

destinationSheetId.description: The ID of the destination sheet

pasteType.description: What to paste (default: NORMAL - all data)

tools.comment: Merges a range of cells in a Google Spreadsheet using the specified merge type.

mergeType.description: How to merge cells: MERGE_ALL (all cells), MERGE_COLUMNS (merge columns), MERGE_ROWS (merge rows)

tools.comment: Unmerges previously merged cells in a specified range of a Google Spreadsheet.
Updates the border styles (top, bottom, left, right, inner) for a range of cells in a Google Spreadsheet.
Applies font, color, alignment, and other formatting to a range of cells in a Google Spreadsheet.
Sorts a range of data in a Google Spreadsheet according to one or more sort specifications.
Creates a new Google Spreadsheet with a specified title and optional initial sheets.

title.description: The title of the new spreadsheet

tools.comment: Duplicates an existing sheet within the same Google Spreadsheet, optionally naming and positioning the copy.

sourceSheetId.description: The ID of the sheet to duplicate

newSheetName.description: The name for the duplicated sheet (optional)

insertSheetIndex.description: The index where the new sheet should be inserted (optional)

tools.comment: Renames a sheet tab in a Google Spreadsheet to a new title.

sheetId.description: The ID of the sheet to rename

newTitle.description: The new title for the sheet

tools.comment: Clears data from multiple specified ranges in a Google Spreadsheet in a single batch operation.

ranges.description: Array of A1 notation ranges to clear (e.g., ['Sheet1!A1:B10', 'Sheet2!C5:D20'])

tools.comment: Automatically resizes rows or columns in a Google Spreadsheet to fit their content.

dimension.description: Whether to resize rows or columns

startIndex.description: The starting index (0-based) of rows/columns to resize

endIndex.description: The ending index (exclusive, 0-based) of rows/columns to resize

tools.comment: Sets the number format (currency, percentage, date, etc.) for a range of cells in a Google Spreadsheet.
Sets data validation rules (dropdowns, number ranges, custom formulas, etc.) for a range of cells in a Google Spreadsheet.

strict.description: Whether to reject input when validation fails (default: true)

showCustomUi.description: Show dropdown for list validation (default: true)

tools.comment: Adds a conditional formatting rule to a range of cells in a Google Spreadsheet.

value.description: Value for comparison (for single value conditions)

backgroundColor.description: Background color to apply when condition is true

textColor.description: Text color to apply when condition is true

bold.description: Make text bold when condition is true

italic.description: Make text italic when condition is true

tools.comment: Creates a basic filter on a specified range in a Google Spreadsheet.
Adds protection to a range of cells in a Google Spreadsheet to restrict or warn on editing, optionally specifying allowed editors by email.

warningOnly.description: If true, show a warning instead of preventing edits (default: false)

users.description: Email addresses of users who can edit

groups.description: Email addresses of groups who can edit

domainUsersCanEdit.description: Whether all users in the domain can edit

editors.description: Who can edit the protected range

tools.comment: Removes protection from a specified protected range in a spreadsheet.

protectedRangeId.description: The ID of the protected range to delete

tools.comment: Updates the protection settings, description, and editors for a protected range in a spreadsheet.

protectedRangeId.description: The ID of the protected range to update

description.description: New description for the protected range

warningOnly.description: If true, show a warning instead of preventing edits

users.description: Email addresses of users who can edit

groups.description: Email addresses of groups who can edit

domainUsersCanEdit.description: Whether all users in the domain can edit

editors.description: Who can edit the protected range

tools.comment: Finds and replaces text within a specified range or entire sheet in a spreadsheet.

sheetId.description: The ID of the sheet (optional, searches all sheets if omitted)

tools.comment: Creates a named range with a specified name and cell boundaries in a spreadsheet.
Updates the name or cell boundaries of an existing named range in a spreadsheet.

namedRangeId.description: The ID of the named range to update

tools.comment: Deletes a named range from a spreadsheet by its ID.

namedRangeId.description: The ID of the named range to delete

tools.comment: Freezes a specified number of rows at the top of a sheet.

count.description: Number of rows to freeze (0 to unfreeze all)

tools.comment: Freezes a specified number of columns at the left of a sheet.

count.description: Number of columns to freeze (0 to unfreeze all)

tools.comment: Adds a new chart of a specified type to a sheet using data from a defined range.

sheetId.description: The ID of the sheet where the chart will be placed

chartType.description: The type of chart to create

dataSheetId.description: The sheet ID containing the data

tools.comment: Updates the title or position of an existing chart in a spreadsheet.

chartId.description: The ID of the chart to update

tools.comment: Deletes a chart from a spreadsheet by its chart ID.

chartId.description: The ID of the chart to delete

tools.comment: Adds a histogram chart to a sheet using data from a specified range.

sheetId.description: The ID of the sheet where the chart will be placed

dataSheetId.description: The sheet ID containing the data

outlierPercentile.description: Percentile to use for outlier detection (optional, 0-1)

tools.comment: Adds a waterfall chart to a sheet using data from a specified range.

sheetId.description: The ID of the sheet where the chart will be placed

dataSheetId.description: The sheet ID containing the data

firstValueIsTotal.description: Whether the first value should be treated as a total (optional)

tools.comment: Adds a candlestick chart for financial data with open/high/low/close values to a sheet.

sheetId.description: The ID of the sheet where the chart will be placed

dataSheetId.description: The sheet ID containing the data

tools.comment: Adds a combo chart combining multiple chart types (e.g., column and line) to a sheet.

sheetId.description: The ID of the sheet where the chart will be placed

dataSheetId.description: The sheet ID containing the data

seriesTypes.description: Array of chart types for each series (optional, defaults to COLUMN for all)

tools.comment: Adds a bubble chart displaying 3D data with bubble size to a sheet.

sheetId.description: The ID of the sheet where the chart will be placed

dataSheetId.description: The sheet ID containing the data

sizeColumn.description: Column index for bubble size values (0-based)

tools.comment: Adds a treemap chart displaying hierarchical data as nested rectangles to a sheet.

sheetId.description: The ID of the sheet where the chart will be placed

dataSheetId.description: The sheet ID containing the data

parentLabelsColumn.description: Column index for parent labels (0-based, optional)

colorColumn.description: Column index for color values (0-based, optional)

tools.comment: Adds an organizational chart displaying hierarchical relationships to a sheet.

sheetId.description: The ID of the sheet where the chart will be placed

dataSheetId.description: The sheet ID containing the data

parentLabelsColumn.description: Column index for parent labels (0-based, optional for root nodes)

tools.comment: Creates a named filter view over a specified range in a spreadsheet.
Creates a pivot table in a spreadsheet from a source data range with specified row groups, column groups, and aggregated values.

sourceSheetId.description: The sheet ID containing source data

targetSheetId.description: The sheet ID where pivot table will be placed

sortOrder.description: Sort order for this row group

showTotals.description: Show totals for this row group

sortOrder.description: Sort order for this column group

showTotals.description: Show totals for this column group

values.description: Values to aggregate in pivot table

tools.comment: Adds a collapsible row or column group to a Google Sheet.

dimension.description: The dimension to group (ROWS or COLUMNS)

startIndex.description: The start index of the dimension group (inclusive)

endIndex.description: The end index of the dimension group (exclusive)

tools.comment: Deletes a row or column group from a Google Sheet.

dimension.description: The dimension of the group to delete (ROWS or COLUMNS)

startIndex.description: The start index of the dimension group to delete (inclusive)

endIndex.description: The end index of the dimension group to delete (exclusive)

tools.comment: Updates properties such as collapse state of a row or column group in a Google Sheet.

dimension.description: The dimension of the group (ROWS or COLUMNS)

startIndex.description: The start index of the dimension group (inclusive)

endIndex.description: The end index of the dimension group (exclusive)

collapsed.description: Whether the group should be collapsed

tools.comment: Creates custom key-value developer metadata attached to a spreadsheet, sheet, row, or column.

location.description: The location where the metadata should be attached

sheetId.description: The sheet ID (required for SHEET, ROW, COLUMN)

dimensionRange.description: The dimension range (required for ROW, COLUMN)

visibility.description: The visibility of the metadata (DOCUMENT or PROJECT)

tools.comment: Updates the key, value, or visibility of existing developer metadata by its ID.

metadataId.description: The ID of the metadata to update

tools.comment: Deletes developer metadata from a spreadsheet by its metadata ID.

metadataId.description: The ID of the metadata to delete

tools.comment: Creates a new Google Document with the specified title.

title.description: The title of the new document

tools.comment: Reads and returns the content of a Google Document by its ID or URL.

documentId.description: Document ID or full Google Docs URL (e.g., https://docs.google.com/document/d/DOCUMENT_ID/edit)

includeFormatting.description: Whether to include text formatting information (default: false)

tools.comment: Retrieves metadata (title, revision ID, document style) for a Google Document.

documentId.description: Document ID or full Google Docs URL (e.g., https://docs.google.com/document/d/DOCUMENT_ID/edit)

tools.comment: Lists Google Documents from Drive, optionally filtered by a search query.

query.description: Search query to filter documents (e.g., 'name contains "report"'). Default: list all documents

pageToken.description: Token for pagination to get the next page of results

pageSize.description: Maximum number of documents to return (default: 10, max: 100)

tools.comment: Inserts text at a specific position in a Google Document.

documentId.description: Document ID or full Google Docs URL

index.description: Position to insert text (1-based index, 1 = start of document)

tools.comment: Replaces text within a specific range in a Google Document.

documentId.description: Document ID or full Google Docs URL

startIndex.description: Start position of text to replace (1-based, inclusive)

endIndex.description: End position of text to replace (1-based, exclusive)

text.description: New text to replace the range with

tools.comment: Deletes text within a specific range from a Google Document.

documentId.description: Document ID or full Google Docs URL

startIndex.description: Start position of text to delete (1-based, inclusive)

endIndex.description: End position of text to delete (1-based, exclusive)

tools.comment: Finds and replaces all occurrences of a text string in a Google Document.

documentId.description: Document ID or full Google Docs URL

tools.comment: Appends text to the end of a Google Document.

documentId.description: Document ID or full Google Docs URL

text.description: Text to append to the end of the document

tools.comment: Applies text formatting (bold, italic, font, color, etc.) to a range in a Google Document.

documentId.description: Document ID or full Google Docs URL

startIndex.description: Start position of text to format (1-based, inclusive)

endIndex.description: End position of text to format (1-based, exclusive)

tools.comment: Creates a heading of a specified level (H1-H6) at a position in a Google Document.

documentId.description: Document ID or full Google Docs URL

level.description: Heading level (1-6, where 1 is H1, 2 is H2, etc.)

index.description: Position to insert heading (1-based). If not provided, appends to end.

tools.comment: Converts a range of paragraphs into a bulleted or numbered list in a Google Document.

documentId.description: Document ID or full Google Docs URL

startIndex.description: Start position of text to convert to list (1-based, inclusive)

endIndex.description: End position of text to convert to list (1-based, exclusive)

nestingLevel.description: Nesting level of the list (0-8, default: 0)

tools.comment: Sets the paragraph alignment for a range of text in a Google Document.

documentId.description: Document ID or full Google Docs URL

startIndex.description: Start position of paragraph to align (1-based, inclusive)

endIndex.description: End position of paragraph to align (1-based, exclusive)

tools.comment: Applies a named paragraph style (Normal, Heading 1-6, Title, Subtitle) to a range in a Google Document.

documentId.description: Document ID or full Google Docs URL

startIndex.description: Start position of paragraph to style (1-based, inclusive)

endIndex.description: End position of paragraph to style (1-based, exclusive)

tools.comment: Inserts an image from a URL at a specific position in a Google Document.

documentId.description: Document ID or full Google Docs URL

imageUrl.description: URL of the image to insert (must be publicly accessible)

tools.comment: Creates a table with specified rows and columns at a position in a Google Document.

documentId.description: Document ID or full Google Docs URL

rows.description: Number of rows in the table

columns.description: Number of columns in the table

tools.comment: Inserts a page break at a specific position in a Google Document.

documentId.description: Document ID or full Google Docs URL

tools.comment: Inserts a hyperlink on a text range in a Google Document.

documentId.description: Document ID or full Google Docs URL

startIndex.description: Start position of text to link (1-based, inclusive)

endIndex.description: End position of text to link (1-based, exclusive)

url.description: URL to link to (must start with http:// or https://)

tools.comment: Inserts a 'Table of Contents' heading placeholder at a specific position in a Google Document.

documentId.description: Document ID or full Google Docs URL

tools.comment: Executes multiple update operations on a Google Document in a single API call.

documentId.description: Document ID or full Google Docs URL

requests.description: Array of update requests to execute

params.description: Parameters for the operation (structure depends on type)

tools.comment: Merges multiple Google Documents into a single new or existing target document.

sourceDocumentIds.description: Array of source document IDs or URLs to merge (in order)

targetDocumentId.description: Target document ID or URL to merge into. If not provided, creates a new document.

title.description: Title for the new merged document (only used if targetDocumentId is not provided)

tools.comment: Exports a Google Document to a specified format such as PDF, DOCX, or plain text.

documentId.description: Document ID or full Google Docs URL

tools.comment: Provides information about enabling or disabling suggestion mode in a Google Document (not programmatically controllable via API).

documentId.description: Document ID or full Google Docs URL

enabled.description: true to enable suggestion mode, false to disable

tools.comment: Lists all labels (system and user-created) in the user's Gmail account.
Searches Gmail messages using Gmail query syntax and returns matching message IDs, thread IDs, and URLs.

query.description: Gmail search query using standard Gmail operators (e.g., 'from:user@example.com subject:invoice is:unread')

pageSize.description: Maximum number of results to return (default: 10, max: 100)

pageToken.description: Token for pagination to get the next page of results

tools.comment: Retrieves the full content and metadata of a specific Gmail message by its ID.

messageId.description: The Gmail message ID to retrieve

tools.comment: Retrieves all messages in a Gmail thread (conversation) in chronological order.

threadId.description: The Gmail thread ID to retrieve

tools.comment: Adds or removes labels from a specific Gmail message in a single operation.

messageId.description: The Gmail message ID to modify

addLabelIds.description: Array of label IDs to add (e.g., ['INBOX', 'STARRED', 'Label_123'])

removeLabelIds.description: Array of label IDs to remove (e.g., ['UNREAD', 'SPAM'])

tools.comment: Creates, updates, or deletes Gmail labels with configurable visibility settings.

action.description: The action to perform on the label

name.description: Label name (required for create, optional for update). The display name of the label.

labelId.description: Label ID (required for update and delete operations). Get this from gmail_list_labels.

labelListVisibility.description: Whether to show the label in the label list (optional for create/update)

messageListVisibility.description: Whether to show the label in the message list (optional for create/update)

tools.comment: Sends an email message immediately via Gmail with support for plain text or HTML, CC/BCC, and threading.

to.description: Recipient email address(es). Multiple addresses can be comma-separated.

body.description: Email body content (plain text or HTML)

cc.description: CC recipient(s). Multiple addresses can be comma-separated.

bcc.description: BCC recipient(s). Multiple addresses can be comma-separated.

threadId.description: Thread ID to reply to (makes this a reply in an existing conversation)

inReplyTo.description: Message-ID header of the message being replied to (for threading)

references.description: References header for email threading (space-separated Message-IDs)

tools.comment: Creates a draft email message in Gmail that is saved but not sent, supporting plain text or HTML and threading.

body.description: Email body content (plain text or HTML)

to.description: Recipient email address(es). Multiple addresses can be comma-separated.

cc.description: CC recipient(s). Multiple addresses can be comma-separated.

bcc.description: BCC recipient(s). Multiple addresses can be comma-separated.

threadId.description: Thread ID to reply to (makes this a reply draft in an existing conversation)

inReplyTo.description: Message-ID header of the message being replied to (for threading)

references.description: References header for email threading (space-separated Message-IDs)

tools.comment: Retrieves multiple Gmail messages in a single batch request for efficiency.

messageIds.description: Array of Gmail message IDs to retrieve (max recommended: 100)

format.description: Response format: 'full' includes body content, 'metadata' only headers (default: metadata)

tools.comment: Retrieves multiple Gmail threads in a single batch request, returning all messages in each thread.

threadIds.description: Array of Gmail thread IDs to retrieve (max recommended: 50)

tools.comment: Adds or removes labels from multiple Gmail messages in a single batch operation.

messageIds.description: Array of Gmail message IDs to modify (max recommended: 1000)

addLabelIds.description: Array of label IDs to add to all specified messages (e.g., ['INBOX', 'STARRED'])

removeLabelIds.description: Array of label IDs to remove from all specified messages (e.g., ['UNREAD', 'SPAM'])

tools.comment: Lists events from a Google Calendar with optional filtering by time range, search query, and pagination.

calendarId.description: Calendar identifier (default: 'primary' for user's main calendar)

timeMin.description: Lower bound (inclusive) for event start time (ISO 8601 format, e.g., '2024-01-01T00:00:00Z')

timeMax.description: Upper bound (exclusive) for event end time (ISO 8601 format)

q.description: Free text search terms to find events matching title, description, location, attendee emails, etc.

maxResults.description: Maximum number of events to return (default: 10, max: 250)

pageToken.description: Token for pagination to get the next page of results

singleEvents.description: Whether to expand recurring events into instances (default: true)

orderBy.description: Order of events: 'startTime' (chronological) or 'updated' (modification time). Requires singleEvents=true for 'startTime'

tools.comment: Retrieves detailed information about a specific calendar event by its ID.

calendarId.description: Calendar identifier (default: 'primary' for user's main calendar)

tools.comment: Creates a new event in Google Calendar with title, time, location, attendees, and optional Google Meet link.

calendarId.description: Calendar identifier (default: 'primary' for user's main calendar)

startTime.description: Event start time (ISO 8601 format: '2024-01-15T10:00:00Z' or date only: '2024-01-15' for all-day)

endTime.description: Event end time (ISO 8601 format: '2024-01-15T11:00:00Z' or date only: '2024-01-16' for all-day)

description.description: Event description (supports plain text and HTML)

sendUpdates.description: Whether to send event notifications: 'all' (send to all attendees), 'externalOnly' (external only), 'none' (no notifications). Default: 'none'

conferenceData.description: Whether to create a Google Meet conference link (default: false)

tools.comment: Updates an existing calendar event's properties, only modifying the specified fields.

calendarId.description: Calendar identifier (default: 'primary' for user's main calendar)

startTime.description: New event start time (ISO 8601 format: '2024-01-15T10:00:00Z' or date only: '2024-01-15' for all-day)

endTime.description: New event end time (ISO 8601 format: '2024-01-15T11:00:00Z' or date only: '2024-01-16' for all-day)

attendees.description: New list of attendee email addresses (replaces existing attendees)

sendUpdates.description: Whether to send event notifications: 'all', 'externalOnly', 'none'. Default: 'all'

tools.comment: Permanently deletes a calendar event with an option to notify attendees of the cancellation.

calendarId.description: Calendar identifier (default: 'primary' for user's main calendar)

sendUpdates.description: Whether to send event cancellation notifications: 'all', 'externalOnly', 'none'. Default: 'all'

tools.comment: Queries free/busy availability for one or more calendars within a specified time range.

timeMin.description: Start of the time range to query (ISO 8601 format, e.g., '2024-01-01T00:00:00Z')

timeMax.description: End of the time range to query (ISO 8601 format)

calendars.description: List of calendar IDs to query (default: ['primary'])

timeZone.description: Time zone for the query (e.g., 'America/New_York', 'UTC'). Default: UTC

tools.comment: Lists all calendars in the user's calendar list with metadata and access roles, supporting pagination and filtering.

maxResults.description: Maximum number of calendars to return per page (default: 100, max: 250)

pageToken.description: Token for accessing subsequent result pages

tools.comment: Retrieves detailed metadata about a specific calendar from the user's calendar list.

calendarId.description: Calendar identifier. Use 'primary' for the user's primary calendar.

tools.comment: Creates a calendar event from a natural language text description using Google Calendar's quick-add parsing.

calendarId.description: Calendar identifier (default: 'primary' for user's main calendar)

text.description: Natural language text describing the event (e.g., 'Dinner with Sarah tomorrow at 7pm')

sendUpdates.description: Whether to send event notifications: 'all', 'externalOnly', 'none'. Default: 'none'

tools.comment: Retrieves all individual instances of a recurring calendar event within an optional time range.

calendarId.description: Calendar identifier (default: 'primary' for user's main calendar)

timeMin.description: Lower bound (inclusive) for instances (RFC3339 format, e.g., '2024-01-15T00:00:00Z')

timeMax.description: Upper bound (exclusive) for instances (RFC3339 format, e.g., '2024-01-31T23:59:59Z')

maxResults.description: Maximum number of instances to return (default: 250, max: 2500)

pageToken.description: Token for accessing subsequent result pages

tools.comment: Moves an event from one calendar to another, removing it from the source and placing it in the destination.

calendarId.description: Source calendar identifier where the event currently exists

destination.description: Destination calendar identifier where the event will be moved to

sendUpdates.description: Whether to send event notifications: 'all', 'externalOnly', 'none'. Default: 'none'

tools.comment: Retrieves the available color definitions (IDs and hex values) for calendars and events.
Creates a new secondary Google Calendar with specified title, description, location, and timezone.
Retrieves metadata for a specific calendar including title, description, location, and timezone.

calendarId.description: Calendar identifier. Use 'primary' for the user's primary calendar.

tools.comment: Updates metadata for an existing calendar such as its title, description, location, and timezone.

calendarId.description: Calendar identifier. Use 'primary' for the user's primary calendar.

location.description: New geographic location as free-form text

timeZone.description: New IANA Time Zone Database name (e.g., 'America/New_York')

tools.comment: Lists all access control rules for a specified calendar, showing who has access and at what permission level.

calendarId.description: Calendar identifier. Use 'primary' for the user's primary calendar.

maxResults.description: Maximum number of ACL entries to return per page (default: 100, max: 250)

pageToken.description: Token for accessing subsequent result pages

tools.comment: Adds a new access control rule to share a calendar with a user, group, or domain at a specified permission level.

calendarId.description: Calendar identifier. Use 'primary' for the user's primary calendar.

scopeValue.description: Email address (for user/group) or domain name (for domain). Omit for 'default' scope type.

sendNotifications.description: Whether to send email notifications about the sharing change (default: true)

tools.comment: Lists all user settings for the authenticated user's Google Calendar, such as timezone, date format, and other preferences.

maxResults.description: Maximum number of settings to return per page (default: 100, max: 250)

pageToken.description: Token for accessing subsequent result pages
